description_long: This is a rustic, Western-style bar counter constructed primarily from dark, textured wood panels arranged in a geometric pattern. The front facade features six square panels, each with a crosshatched design, and a raised back section with a prominent 'BAR' sign above a central mirror. Two decorative rifles are mounted on either side of the mirror, enhancing the Western saloon aesthetic. The countertop displays a few green glass bottles and a notepad with a pen, suggesting a functional serving area. Brass foot rails run along the bottom front, providing comfort for patrons. The overall design is compact and detailed, evoking the atmosphere of a classic Old West saloon.
description: A rustic Western-style bar counter with wood paneling, a 'BAR' sign, decorative rifles, and a mirrored back. The countertop features bottles and a notepad, with brass foot rails at the base.
description_view_0: Rear view showing the geometric wood paneling and the raised back section, with the 'BAR' sign partially visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the bar, the thickness of the countertop, and the protruding brass foot rail.
description_view_2: Front view displaying the 'BAR' sign, mirror, decorative rifles, bottles, notepad, and the full arrangement of wood panels and brass foot rail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the countertop items and the bar's layered construction.
attribute_signage: Raised block letters spelling 'BAR' mounted above the mirror, made of the same wood as the bar, unlit and integrated into the back panel.
